sections.title: Output Types Covered
sections.content:
| ID | Output Type | L5 Product |
|---|---|---|
| OT-1 | Lager / Pilsner | Light-colored, bottom-fermented, filtered, carbonated packaged beer |
| OT-2 | Ale (IPA, Pale, Amber) | Top-fermented, varied hop profile, packaged |
| OT-3 | Stout / Porter | Dark, roasted-malt dominant, top-fermented |
| OT-4 | Wheat Beer | ≥50% wheat malt, top-fermented, hazy or filtered |
| OT-5 | Non-alcoholic Beer | Dealcoholized or arrested-fermentation malt beverage |
| OT-6 | Hard Seltzer / FMB | Fermented sugar base, flavored, carbonated, low-calorie |
| OT-7 | Sour / Wild Ale | Mixed fermentation, acid-dominant, barrel/foudre aged |

sections.title: L4 Subsystem Matrix
sections.content:

sections.title: L4 → L0 Decomposition (Generic — Cross-Output-Type Reference)
sections.content:
### L4.1 — Fermented Liquid Assembly

```
L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
├── L3.1  Base Water (brewing liquor — L0)
├── L3.2  Fermentable Substrate (malt, adjuncts, sugar)
├── L3.3  Bittering & Aromatic Agent (hops / derivatives)
├── L3.4  Fermentation Agent (yeast)
└── L3.5  Adjunct / Flavor Additions (stop at L1)
```

#### L3.1 Base Water (L0)

Water = 90–94% of finished beer by weight. No substitutable alternative at this position — stop at L0. Ion-profile additives (gypsum, CaCl₂, lactic acid) are process inputs, not product BOM constituents.

**Sensor relevance:** Brewing liquor flow through mash tun, wort copper, and CIP circuits = primary Marquardt measurement window (VN L6.7–L6.8).

#### L3.2 Fermentable Substrate — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | OT | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Barley malt (2-row / 6-row base malt) | 62% | Stable | All beer OTs | HIGH | Brewers Association 2024 |
| Corn adjunct (flakes, grits, syrup) | 10% | Stable | OT-1 macro lager | HIGH | USDA |
| Wheat malt | 8% | ↑ Growing | OT-4, OT-2 hefeweizen | HIGH | BA 2024 |
| Specialty / crystal / roasted malt (added to base) | 8% | Stable | OT-3 (heavy), OT-2 | HIGH | BA 2024 |
| Rice adjunct | 5% | Stable | OT-1 (Anheuser-Busch profile) | HIGH | USDA |
| Sugar (sucrose / dextrose) | 5% | ↑ Growing | OT-5, OT-6 | HIGH | BA 2024 |
| Alternative grains (oats, rye, sorghum, millet) | 2% | ↑ Emerging | OT-2, gluten-free | MEDIUM | analyst estimate |

Sum 100%. Shares reflect volume of fermentable material across total US beer production.

#### L3.3 Bittering & Aromatic Agent — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hop pellets T-90 (compressed whole-leaf) | 55% | ↓ Slight decline | HIGH | Barth-Haas Hop Report 2024 |
| Hop extracts (CO₂ extract / isomerized alpha-acid) | 28% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Barth-Haas 2024 |
| Whole cone hops (fresh / dried) | 10% | Stable | MEDIUM | craft segment data |
| Cryo / LupuLN₂ hop products (concentrated lupulin) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | analyst estimate |
| Non-hop bittering agents (gentian, wormwood, grains of paradise) | 2% | Stable niche | LOW | — |

Sum 100%.

**L2 → L0 for hop extract path:** Hop flower (L1) → alpha-acid resin (L0 organic compound). Stop here; no further commercially relevant alternatives.

#### L3.4 Fermentation Agent — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* (ale, top-fermenting) | 45% | Stable | HIGH |
| *Saccharomyces pastorianus* (lager, bottom-fermenting) | 45% | Stable | HIGH |
| *Brettanomyces* / mixed-culture (sour / wild ale) | 5% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM |
| Kveik strains (Norwegian farmhouse) | 3% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM |
| Non-*Saccharomyces* / adjunct yeast blends | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW |

Sum 100%. Lager dominates by volume; ale by SKU count.

#### L3.5 Adjunct / Flavor Additions — Stop at L1

Fruit purées, lactose, vanilla, coffee, spices, flavoring extracts: commodity ingredients. No single alternative drives structural BOM change. Stop at L1.

---

### L4.2 — Carbonation Module (L0)

CO₂ dissolved into beer post-fermentation. Alternatives at **source supply** only:

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Industrial bulk CO₂ (truck delivery, liquid) | 50% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | industry reports 2024 |
| Recovered fermentation CO₂ (on-site capture + purification) | 45% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | CO₂ recovery ROI reports 2024 |
| Nitrogen blend (widget / Guinness-type stout dispensing) | 5% | Stable | HIGH | — |

Sum 100%. Recovery trend driven by CO₂ cost spikes (post-2022) and sustainability mandates.

**Sensor relevance:** CO₂ recovery systems require precise flow control on carbonation dosing lines — secondary Marquardt opportunity.

---

### L4.3 — Stabilization & Clarity

```
L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity
├── L3.6  Filtration
├── L3.7  Fining Agent (L0 — chemical substance)
└── L3.8  Antioxidant Treatment (SO₂, ascorbic acid — stop at L0)
```

#### L3.6 Filtration — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Kieselguhr (diatomaceous earth) depth filtration | 45% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Brewing industry reports 2024 |
| Sheet / cartridge filtration (cellulose or perlite) | 25% | Stable | HIGH | — |
| Cross-flow / membrane filtration (ceramic or polymer) | 20% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | GEA / Pall Corp product data |
| Centrifugal separation (no filter media) | 8% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Alfa Laval/GEA market data |
| None — hazy / unfiltered (craft segment) | 2% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Brewers Association craft trends |

Sum 100%. Kieselguhr declining due to waste disposal regulations and health concerns (silica dust).

#### L3.7 Fining Agent (L0) — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Isinglass (fish collagen, post-fermentation) | 35% | MEDIUM |
| Bentonite (montmorillonite clay) | 30% | HIGH |
| Silica gel (kieselsol, colloidal) | 20% | HIGH |
| Carrageenan / Irish moss (whirlpool addition) | 10% | HIGH |
| None (centrifuge / membrane sufficient) | 5% | MEDIUM |

Sum 100%.

---

### L4.4 — Primary Packaging Assembly

```
L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
├── L3.9  Primary Container
├── L3.10 Closure / Seal
└── L3.11 Label / Print Layer (stop at L1)
```

#### L3.9 Primary Container — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aluminum can (12 oz / 16 oz, 2-piece drawn) | 60% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Beer Institute 2024 |
| Glass bottle (12 oz long-neck / 22 oz) | 25% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Beer Institute 2024 |
| Stainless keg (1/2-bbl, 1/4-bbl, 1/6-bbl) | 13% | Stable | HIGH | NBWA 2024 |
| PET bottle (export / single-serve plastic) | 1% | Stable | MEDIUM | — |
| Bag-in-box / bulk bag (export, event) | 1% | Stable | LOW | — |

Sum 100%.

**L2 → L0 for aluminum can path:**
- L2: Can body + can end assembly
- L1: Drawn aluminum can body; stamped aluminum end (B64 / CDL profile)
- L0: Aluminum alloy sheet (3004-H19 body; 5182-H19 end) — stop at L0

#### L3.10 Closure / Seal — Alternatives

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| Aluminum can lid with pull-tab (B64 / CDL) | 60% | ↑ | HIGH |
| Crown cap (26mm / 29mm, tin-coated steel) | 25% | ↓ | HIGH |
| Keg spear + coupler port (D-type US, S-type Euro) | 13% | Stable | HIGH |
| Swing-top / Bügelverschluss (specialty / export) | 1% | Stable | MEDIUM |
| Screw cap (PET) | 1% | Stable | MEDIUM |

Sum 100%.

---

### L4.5 — Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1

- Paper / pressure-sensitive film label: commodity, stop at L1.
- Corrugated cardboard 4- or 6-pack carrier / 24-pack case: commodity, stop at L1.
- Shrink sleeve (PET film): commodity, stop at L1.

---

sections.title: Marquardt Sensor Position Note
sections.content:
**The Marquardt sensor IS NOT a beer BOM constituent.** It is process equipment, positioned in the VN (how beer is made), not in the product BOM (what beer is made of).

| VN Position | L6 System | What Sensor Measures | Connection to BOM |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Primary** | L6.6 Glycol Cooling | Glycol/water mass flow to fermenter jackets and bright tanks | Controls temperature → yeast metabolite profile → L3.4 quality |
| **Secondary** | L6.7 CIP & Sanitation | Water + caustic + acid flow through vessel circuits | Vessel sterility → L4.3 stability |
| **Tertiary** | L6.8 Water Treatment | Brewing liquor flow to mash tun and kettle | L3.1 water volume / recipe accuracy |

**Critical failure mode:** Glycol flow loss → temperature excursion during fermentation → diacetyl, acetaldehyde, or ester off-flavor in L4.1 → full batch dump (typical loss: $15–80k per fermenter-batch at mid-to-large craft scale). Sensor is the only non-contact, maintenance-free way to detect glycol circuit failures before batch damage occurs.

---

sections.title: Per-Output-Type BOM Trees
sections.content:
> These trees show the specific composition of each output type. Elements that differ materially from the generic cross-output reference above are shown in full. Cross-cutting elements that are identical across all output types (L4.5 label/secondary packaging, standard aluminum can L2 construction) are abbreviated with a note pointing to the generic section.

---

### OT-1: Lager / Pilsner

Bottom-fermented, cold-conditioned (4–8 weeks at 0–4 °C), filtered bright. Two sub-variants: (a) macro lager — 20–30% corn or rice adjunct; (b) craft pilsner — all-barley malt. Pale color (3–7 SRM), low IBU (15–25), high carbonation (2.5–2.8 vol CO₂).

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Lager / Pilsner
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Soft brewing liquor (Ca 40–80 ppm, SO₄ low, Cl moderate — Pilsen/Munich profile)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate
          — alternatives: 2-row barley base malt (craft pilsner) 45%,
            2-row + corn adjunct blend (macro lager) 25%,
            2-row + rice adjunct blend (AB-InBev profile) 20%,
            6-row barley base (North American macro) 8%,
            Gluten-free grain base (rice/millet — emerging) 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Malted grain substrate
        L1  Malted 2-row barley kernel (modified, diastatic; 2–4 EBC)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare, 2-row; kilned pale)
        L1  Corn grits / flakes (gelatinized adjunct path)
          L0  Dent corn (Zea mays var. indentata, food-grade milling fraction)
        L1  Rice grits (parboiled / broken adjunct path)
          L0  Broken milling-grade rice (Oryza sativa)
    L3.3 Bittering & Aromatic Agent
          — alternatives: Noble hop pellets T-90 (Saaz, Hallertau, Tettnang) 50%,
            Isomerized alpha-acid (IAA) liquid extract 30%,
            Pre-isomerized / RHIA / PIKE kettle-free extract 12%,
            Whole cone noble hops (contract) 5%,
            CO₂ supercritical extract (no dry-hop) 3% [HIGH]
      L2  Hop product
        L1  Compressed hop pellet T-90 (8 mm, vacuum-sealed foil pouch)
          L0  Dried hop cone (Humulus lupulus, noble variety; alpha-acid 3–5%)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent
          — alternatives: S. pastorianus Type II / Frohberg strain 65%,
            S. pastorianus Type I / Saaz strain 25%,
            S. cerevisiae hybrid lager (warm-tolerant) 7%,
            Kveik-based lager substitute 3% [HIGH]
      L1  Liquid yeast slurry (cropped/repitched) or dry pitch
        L0  Saccharomyces pastorianus culture (2–5 × 10⁶ cells/mL pitching rate)
    L3.5 Specialty Additions — None (lager = clean profile; L3.5 absent)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (2.5–2.8 vol CO₂)
        — alternatives: Industrial bulk liquid CO₂ 55%,
          Recovered fermentation CO₂ (on-site capture) 42%,
          Nitrogen top-up (headspace only) 3% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ gas (food grade, ≥99.9% purity, <50 ppb acetaldehyde)
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (bright/filtered mandatory for lager)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: Kieselguhr depth filtration (primary + polishing) 55%,
            Sheet/cartridge filtration 22%,
            Cross-flow membrane filtration 15%,
            Centrifugal disc-stack separator 8% [HIGH]
      L2  Filter assembly
        L1  Kieselguhr filter cake (primary filter medium)
          L0  Diatomaceous earth (amorphous SiO₂, food grade, calcined)
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: Silica gel / kieselsol (colloidal SiO₂) 35%,
            Bentonite (Na-montmorillonite clay) 30%,
            PVPP (polyvinylpolypyrrolidone — polyphenol removal) 20%,
            Carrageenan (whirlpool addition) 10%,
            Isinglass 5% [HIGH]
      L0  Colloidal silica / Na-montmorillonite / PVPP polymer
    L3.8 Antioxidant Treatment
      L0  SO₂ (K₂S₂O₅ potassium metabisulfite) and/or ascorbic acid
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum can 12 oz 2-piece DWI 65%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz long-neck (amber) 22%,
            Stainless steel keg (1/2-bbl US = 15.5 gal) 12%,
            PET bottle 1% [HIGH]
      L2  Aluminum can assembly (dominant path)
        L1  Can body (drawn/ironed, 211 diameter, necked + flanged)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 3004-H19 (body stock; ~0.099 mm gauge)
        L1  Can end (B64 or CDL profile, 202 diameter)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 5182-H19 (end stock; ~0.235 mm gauge)
      L2  Glass bottle assembly (alternate path)
        L1  Blown amber glass long-neck 12 oz
          L0  Soda-lime-silica glass (SiO₂ 70–74%; Fe₂O₃ colorant → amber)
    L3.10 Closure / Seal
          — alternatives: Aluminum pull-tab lid (can path) 65%,
            Crown cap 26mm tin-coated steel + LDPE liner (bottle) 22%,
            Keg spear + D-type coupler port 12%,
            Screw cap (PET only) 1% [HIGH]
      L1  Crown cap (bottle path)
        L0  Tin-coated low-carbon steel sheet; LDPE plastisol liner
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1
    L1  Pressure-sensitive label (OPP film or paper)
    L1  4-/6-pack carrier (recyclable corrugated or HDPE ring)
    L1  24-pack corrugated shipper (B/C flute)
```

---

### OT-2: Ale (IPA / Pale Ale / Amber Ale)

Top-fermented (15–22 °C), warm-conditioned 1–2 weeks. High hop presence (30–100+ IBU for IPA). Diverse specialty malts. May be hazy (NEIPA) or filtered bright. Dry-hopping is a defining module absent in all other output types except OT-7.

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Ale (IPA / Pale Ale / Amber Ale)
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Sulfate-forward brewing liquor (Ca 150–200 ppm, SO₄ 200–300 ppm — Burton/London profile)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate
          — alternatives: 2-row pale malt base 100% malt 55%,
            2-row base + crystal/caramel malt blend (amber ales; 60–120 EBC) 25%,
            2-row base + wheat malt ≤20% (haze/body — NEIPA) 10%,
            2-row base + flaked oats (NEIPA texture) 8%,
            Brewer's glucose addition (session ale ABV reduction) 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Malted grain substrate
        L1  Malted 2-row barley pale malt (2–4 EBC)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare, 2-row, fully modified)
        L1  Crystal/caramel malt (amber ales; 60–120 EBC, kilned at green-malt stage)
          L0  Barley grain (caramelized in-husk during kilning; residual sugars)
        L1  Flaked oats (NEIPA path; silky texture, haze contribution)
          L0  Rolled oat grain (Avena sativa, food grade)
    L3.3 Bittering & Aromatic Agent (HIGH hop rate — distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: American pellets T-90 (Citra, Mosaic, Centennial, Cascade) 50%,
            Cryo/LupuLN₂ pellets (concentrated lupulin) 20%,
            CO₂ hop extract (kettle bittering) 15%,
            Whole cone hops (dry-hop specialty) 8%,
            UK/European pellets T-90 (Fuggles, EKG, Challenger) 5%,
            Hop oil / terpene extract (aroma only) 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Hop product (T-90 dominant path)
        L1  Compressed hop pellet T-90 (8 mm, vacuum-sealed foil)
          L0  Dried hop cone (Humulus lupulus, American high-alpha/aroma; alpha 8–16%)
    L3.3a Dry-Hop Module (IPA-specific — present in ~80% of IPAs; absent in lager/stout/wheat)
          — alternatives: Pellet T-90 dry-hop (tank, cold-side 2–7 °C) 60%,
            Cryo/LupuLN₂ dry-hop (reduced plant matter) 25%,
            Hop oil/terpene addition (biotransformation target) 10%,
            Whole cone dry-hop (specialty craft) 5% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Late-addition hop pellets (added post-fermentation)
        L0  Dried hop cone (aroma-forward variety; same path as L3.3 above)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent
          — alternatives: S. cerevisiae US-05 type (clean neutral — pale/IPA) 40%,
            S. cerevisiae English ale strains (fruity esters) 30%,
            S. cerevisiae Vermont/London haze yeast (NEIPA) 20%,
            S. cerevisiae Belgian strains (high ester/phenol) 7%,
            Kveik strains (high-temp ale) 3% [HIGH]
      L1  Liquid yeast pitch (propagated) or dry yeast
        L0  Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture (0.75–1.0 × 10⁶ cells/mL/°P pitching rate)
    L3.5 Specialty Additions — Stop at L1 (fruit/lactose/vanilla in adjunct sub-styles)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (2.2–2.8 vol CO₂)
        — alternatives: Industrial bulk CO₂ 52%,
          Recovered fermentation CO₂ 45%,
          Cask-conditioned natural carbonation (real ale) 3% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ food grade ≥99.9% / natural fermentation CO₂
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: None — unfiltered/unfined (NEIPA; haze intentional) 30%,
            Sheet/cartridge filtration (light polish) 30%,
            Kieselguhr depth filtration (clear ale) 25%,
            Cross-flow membrane 10%,
            Centrifugal disc-stack 5% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: None (NEIPA — haze desired) 30%,
            Isinglass (English ale tradition) 25%,
            Bentonite 20%,
            Silica gel / kieselsol 15%,
            Irish moss / carrageenan (whirlpool) 10% [HIGH]
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum can 12 oz / 16 oz (craft skew) 68%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz / 22 oz bomber 20%,
            Stainless keg 1/6-bbl to 1/2-bbl 11%,
            Growler/crowler (on-site) 1% [HIGH]
      L2  Aluminum can assembly
        L1  Can body (211 diameter drawn/ironed)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 3004-H19
        L1  Can end (CDL profile 202 — wide-mouth pour preferred)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 5182-H19
    L3.10 Closure / Seal — same alternatives as OT-1 (can lid / crown cap / keg spear)
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1 (same as OT-1)
```

---

### OT-3: Stout / Porter

Top-fermented, dark (30–80+ SRM), roasted/bitter. Key structural differentiators: heavy roasted malt module in L3.2, optional lactose (milk stout), nitrogen carbonation option (Guinness widget), and a meaningful keg share because stout is predominantly a draught product.

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Stout / Porter
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Soft-to-moderate water (Dublin profile: Ca 120 ppm, Cl 190 ppm, low SO₄)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate (ROASTED MALT — distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives:
            Base malt 60% + roasted barley 15% + chocolate malt 15% + black patent 10% (dry stout) 35%,
            Base malt 65% + chocolate malt 20% + crystal 10% + flaked oats 5% (oatmeal stout) 30%,
            Base malt 70% + brown malt 15% + crystal 10% + chocolate malt 5% (porter) 20%,
            Base malt 65% + lactose 10% + chocolate malt 15% + roasted barley 10% (milk stout) 10%,
            Imperial high-gravity bill + adjuncts (imperial stout) 5% [HIGH]
      L2  Malted grain substrate (roasted path dominant)
        L1  Roasted barley (500–600 EBC; unmalted, drum-roasted — dry stout)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare; roasted un-malted for coffee/dry character)
        L1  Chocolate malt (250–450 EBC; malted barley drum-roasted — porter/stout)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare, fully modified base malt; drum-roasted)
        L1  Flaked oats (oatmeal stout path; silky body)
          L0  Oat grain (Avena sativa, rolled, food grade)
        L1  Lactose powder (milk stout path; non-fermentable disaccharide)
          L0  Lactose (C₁₂H₂₂O₁₁; whey-derived dairy by-product, food grade)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent (subordinate role — low IBU 20–40)
          — alternatives: English hop pellets T-90 (Fuggles, East Kent Goldings) 55%,
            CO₂ extract / IAA (bittering only, no aroma target) 30%,
            American pellets (craft stout) 10%,
            Whole cone English hops 5% [HIGH]
      L1  Pellet T-90 (English variety, kettle addition only)
        L0  Dried hop cone (Humulus lupulus; Fuggles or EKG; alpha 4–6%)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent
          — alternatives: S. cerevisiae Irish/English ale strains (Wyeast 1084 type) 60%,
            S. cerevisiae US clean ale strain (craft stout) 30%,
            S. cerevisiae Belgian high-gravity strain (imperial stout) 8%,
            Mixed fermentation (barrel-aged sour stout) 2% [HIGH]
      L1  Liquid yeast pitch or dry yeast
        L0  Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture (Irish/English ale phenotype)
    L3.5 Specialty Additions (stout-specific)
          — alternatives: None (traditional dry stout) 50%,
            Lactose (milk stout — kettle, non-fermentable) 25%,
            Coffee/espresso extract (craft adjunct) 10%,
            Vanilla extract/nibs 8%,
            Chocolate/cocoa nibs 7% [HIGH]
      L1  Lactose (milk stout — powder or liquid syrup, kettle addition)
        L0  Lactose (whey-derived; dairy industry by-product)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (NITROGEN option — distinguishing feature)
        — alternatives: CO₂ dissolved (2.0–2.5 vol) — cans/bottles 55%,
          CO₂/N₂ blend draught — 75:25 N₂:CO₂ (creamy head) 35%,
          Nitrogen widget system (canned nitro — hollow HDPE ball) 10% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ gas (food grade) + liquid nitrogen (food grade, N₂ ≥99.9%)
    L1  Nitrogen widget (hollow polyethylene ball ⌀ ~20 mm — nitro can path)
      L0  HDPE (high-density polyethylene, food contact grade)
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (stout often served with haze; lighter filtration)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: Light sheet/cartridge filtration 45%,
            None — natural sedimentation (craft bottle-conditioned) 25%,
            Kieselguhr filtration (production volume stout) 20%,
            Centrifugal disc-stack only 10% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: Isinglass (English tradition) 40%,
            None (stout haze tolerated) 30%,
            Bentonite 20%,
            Irish moss / carrageenan (whirlpool) 10% [HIGH]
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container (KEGS relatively large share — draught dominance)
          — alternatives: Aluminum can standard (non-nitro) 35%,
            Stainless keg 1/2-bbl or 1/6-bbl (draught stout) 30%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz / 22 oz bomber 18%,
            Aluminum can — nitro widget 15%,
            PET bottle 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Nitrogen widget can system (nitro path)
        L1  Aluminum can body + end (same as standard; see OT-1 L2)
          L0  Aluminum alloy 3004-H19 / 5182-H19
        L1  Nitrogen widget (hollow HDPE ball ~20 mm diameter)
          L0  HDPE (high-density polyethylene)
        L0  Liquid nitrogen charge (20–30 μL pressurized fill inside widget)
    L3.10 Closure / Seal — same alternatives as OT-1 per container type
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1 (same as OT-1)
```

---

### OT-4: Wheat Beer (Hefeweizen / Witbier / American Wheat)

≥50% wheat malt. Top-fermented with specialized yeast producing phenolic (4-vinyl guaiacol / clove) and ester (isoamyl acetate / banana) character. Traditional Hefeweizen is served unfiltered and hazy; Kristallweizen is filtered bright. Belgian Witbier adds coriander and orange peel in L3.5. Bottle conditioning is relatively common (40%+).

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Wheat Beer (Hefeweizen / Witbier / American Wheat)
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Soft water (Munich/Belgian profile; Ca 30–60 ppm, SO₄ very low, Cl elevated — enhances wheat softness)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate (WHEAT dominant — ≥50% wheat malt; distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: Malted wheat ≥50% + 2-row barley base (German Hefeweizen) 45%,
            Torrefied/raw wheat ≥30% + oats + barley (Belgian Witbier) 30%,
            Malted wheat 30–50% + 2-row barley (American wheat — filtered) 20%,
            Malted wheat + rye addition (Roggenweizen — specialty) 5% [HIGH]
      L2  Malted grain substrate
        L1  Malted wheat kernel (pale wheat malt, 2–5 EBC)
          L0  Wheat grain (Triticum aestivum, brewing variety; malted to develop enzymes)
        L1  2-row barley base malt (remainder of grist)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare, 2-row)
        L1  Torrefied / flaked wheat (Witbier path — raw starch for haze contribution)
          L0  Wheat grain (raw, heat-popped/torrefied for starch gelatinization)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent (LOW hop rate — subordinate role; 10–15 IBU)
          — alternatives: German noble pellets T-90 (Hallertau, Perle, Tradition) 60%,
            CO₂ extract / IAA (bittering only, no aroma) 28%,
            Whole cone German hops (traditional) 10%,
            Belgian Styrian Goldings or Saaz equivalent 2% [HIGH]
      L1  Hop pellets T-90 (kettle bittering only; no dry-hop in any wheat style)
        L0  Dried hop cone (noble variety, alpha 3–6%)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent (SPECIALIZED YEAST — distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: S. cerevisiae Hefeweizen strain (high 4VG + banana ester; POF+) 50%,
            S. cerevisiae Witbier/Belgian wheat strain (spicy/phenolic) 25%,
            S. cerevisiae American wheat strain (clean, neutral; POF−) 20%,
            S. cerevisiae Berliner wheat strain (mild acid production) 5% [HIGH]
      L1  Liquid yeast pitch (Hefeweizen yeast — Wyeast 3068 / WLP300 phenotype)
        L0  S. cerevisiae Hefeweizen culture (high 4-vinyl guaiacol phenol-positive strain)
    L3.5 Specialty Additions (Witbier path only — absent in Hefeweizen)
          — alternatives: None (German Hefeweizen — yeast character only) 45%,
            Dried curaçao orange peel + coriander seeds (Belgian Witbier) 40%,
            Dried orange peel only (Witbier variant) 8%,
            Chamomile or other botanicals (craft Belgian wheat) 7% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Dried curaçao orange peel (Citrus aurantium var. amara — Witbier path)
        L0  Dried bitter orange peel (bitter citrus essential oil: limonene, linalool)
      L1  Whole coriander seeds (lightly cracked, whirlpool addition)
        L0  Coriander seed (Coriandrum sativum; linalool, geraniol volatiles)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (2.8–4.0 vol CO₂ — HIGHER than other styles)
        — alternatives: Bottle conditioning — natural CO₂ from residual yeast (traditional Hefe) 40%,
          Industrial / recovered CO₂ (filtered Kristallweizen) 50%,
          Yeast rousing / krausening 10% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ (fermentation-derived or industrial food grade)
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (HAZY intentional for Hefeweizen)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: None — yeast in suspension (traditional Hefeweizen) 50%,
            Light sheet filtration (American wheat / Kristallweizen) 30%,
            Centrifugal disc-stack (partial clarification only) 15%,
            Kieselguhr (export / shelf-stable wheat) 5% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: None (Hefeweizen — haze desired; no fining) 50%,
            Irish moss / carrageenan (whirlpool — hot break only) 30%,
            Bentonite (filtered American wheat) 15%,
            Isinglass 5% [MEDIUM]
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum can 12 oz / 16 oz (US craft wheat) 40%,
            Glass bottle 500 mL tall/narrow (German Hefe tradition) 35%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz (US craft) 15%,
            Stainless keg (draught) 9%,
            PET bottle 1% [MEDIUM]
      L2  Tall glass bottle assembly (German tradition path)
        L1  Blown glass 500 mL long-neck (clear or amber, tall profile)
          L0  Soda-lime-silica glass (clear: low iron SiO₂; tall mold profile)
    L3.10 Closure / Seal
          — alternatives: Crown cap 26 mm (all bottle formats) 50%,
            Aluminum pull-tab lid (can format) 40%,
            Keg spear / coupler (keg format) 9%,
            Swing-top / Bügelverschluss (Bavarian specialty export) 1% [HIGH]
      L1  Swing-top stopper assembly (specialty path)
        L1  Porcelain stopper (Bügel)
          L0  Aluminum oxide ceramic (Al₂O₃ slip-cast)
        L1  Rubber gasket (O-ring seal)
          L0  Natural rubber (Hevea brasiliensis; food-grade NR compound)
        L1  Wire bail (swing mechanism)
          L0  Galvanized steel wire (low-carbon; Ø 2.5 mm)
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1 (same as OT-1)
```

### OT-5: Non-Alcoholic Beer (≤0.5% ABV)

Two fundamentally different production routes: **(a) Arrested fermentation** — remove yeast before full attenuation; **(b) Dealcoholization** — brew normal beer then strip alcohol via vacuum distillation, reverse osmosis, or spinning cone column. Route B produces fuller flavor but requires capital-intensive process equipment (VN, not BOM). Post-process body/flavor rebuilding is a critical BOM module unique to this output type.

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Non-Alcoholic Beer (≤0.5% ABV)
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Treated brewing liquor (standard; slightly harder for mouthfeel body compensation)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate (modified for low-alcohol target)
          — alternatives: High-dextrin / low-fermentability malt blend (arrested fermentation path) 40%,
            Standard 2-row base malt (dealcoholization path — full fermentation then strip) 35%,
            Base malt + maltodextrin addition (body builder) 15%,
            Gluten-free grain base (millet, rice, buckwheat) 10% [HIGH]
      L2  Malt substrate
        L1  High-dextrin specialty malt (under-modified kilning; minimal fermentable sugars)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare; under-modified kilning → residual dextrins for body)
        L1  Maltodextrin (body supplement)
          L0  Maltodextrin DE 10–20 (starch hydrolysate; corn or wheat derived)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent
          — alternatives: IAA / isomerized extract (bitter without hop aroma — alcohol strip safe) 55%,
            Noble hop pellets T-90 40%,
            CO₂ hop extract 5% [HIGH]
      L1  IAA liquid extract (no aromatic volatiles; alcohol-stable)
        L0  Isomerized alpha-acid solution (potassium salt of iso-alpha acids; food grade)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent
          — alternatives: Low-attenuation S. cerevisiae (arrested fermentation path) 40%,
            Standard S. pastorianus (lager — dealcoholized path) 30%,
            Standard S. cerevisiae (ale — dealcoholized path) 20%,
            Maltose-negative / non-fermenting yeast (0.0% ABV target) 10% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Yeast slurry / dry pitch (restricted-attenuation strain)
        L0  Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture (low-attenuation or maltose-negative phenotype)
    L3.5 Body & Flavor Restoration Module (NA-SPECIFIC — distinguishing module)
          — alternatives: Malt extract addition (body rebuild post-dealcoholization) 40%,
            Glycerol (humectant, mouthfeel) 20%,
            Natural beer flavor — proprietary blend (Givaudan / IFF / Firmenich) 20%,
            Hop aroma extract (aroma rebuild post-distillation) 15%,
            Acacia gum / gum arabic (body + foam stability) 5% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Malt extract liquid concentrate (spray-dried or vacuum-evaporated wort)
        L0  Evaporated wort (barley malt-derived concentrate; 80° Brix)
      L1  Glycerol (food-grade, kosher/halal certified)
        L0  Glycerol (1,2,3-propanetriol; vegetable-derived or synthetic)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (2.2–2.7 vol CO₂)
        — alternatives: Industrial CO₂ 65%,
          Recovered fermentation CO₂ (limited — minimal fermentation extent) 30%,
          CO₂ + nitrogen blend (low-ABV creamy niche) 5% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ food grade
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (thorough — NA must be highly shelf-stable)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: Cross-flow membrane (preferred — cold, retains aroma) 40%,
            Sheet/cartridge filtration 35%,
            Kieselguhr + polishing 20%,
            Centrifugal + polishing 5% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: PVPP (polyphenol removal — critical for NA oxidation stability) 35%,
            Bentonite 30%,
            Silica gel / kieselsol 25%,
            None 10% [HIGH]
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly (same container mix as lager)
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum can 12 oz / 330 mL 60%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz / 330 mL 28%,
            Stainless keg (foodservice / hospitality) 10%,
            PET bottle (single-serve convenience) 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Aluminum can assembly — same construction as OT-1 (3004-H19 body / 5182-H19 end)
    L3.10 Closure / Seal — same as OT-1 per container type
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1 (same as OT-1)
```

---

### OT-6: Hard Seltzer / FMB (Flavored Malt Beverage)

Fermented from a plain sugar-water solution — no grain malt, no hops. ABV 4–6%. Crystal clear, very low calorie (~100 kcal/355 mL), very high carbonation (3.0–3.5 vol CO₂). Natural fruit flavors added post-fermentation. Slim 12 oz can is the dominant primary container (75%+). Structurally the most different BOM from all other OTs.

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Hard Seltzer / FMB (Flavored Malt Beverage)
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  RO-purified water (ultra-pure; TDS <10 ppm pre-mineral; then Ca/Mg added back minimally)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate (SIMPLE SUGAR BASE — no grain malt; distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: Refined cane sugar (sucrose, dissolved solution) 50%,
            HFCS-55 / corn sugar syrup 25%,
            Dextrose / glucose monohydrate (rapid fermentation) 20%,
            Malt syrup (maltose-based — TTB malt beverage classification) 5% [HIGH]
      L1  Refined cane sugar (granulated or liquid, food grade)
        L0  Sucrose (C₁₂H₂₂O₁₁; sugarcane or sugar beet derived)
      L1  HFCS-55 (liquid, 55% fructose / 42% glucose)
        L0  Corn starch hydrolysate (enzymatic isomerization; maize-derived)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent — ABSENT (no hops in hard seltzer)
          Note: ~3% of FMBs add nominal hop for TTB malt beverage classification (regulatory only, <5 IBU)
          — alternatives: No hop addition 97%, Nominal hop addition (regulatory) 3% [HIGH]
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent (HIGH-ATTENUATING, NEUTRAL YEAST — distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: Wine/champagne yeast (EC-1118 type; very high attenuation) 55%,
            Specially selected brewing yeast (clean, neutral, high attenuation) 30%,
            Distiller's yeast (fast, neutral, high attenuation) 12%,
            Kveik strains (temperature-flexible, high attenuation) 3% [HIGH]
      L1  Dry yeast pitch (wine yeast, EC-1118 or equivalent)
        L0  Saccharomyces cerevisiae culture (dried, high-gravity tolerant, neutral phenotype)
    L3.5 Fruit Flavor & Color Module (POST-FERMENTATION — distinguishing module)
          — alternatives: Natural fruit flavor concentrate (citrus, berry, mango, etc.) 70%,
            Fruit juice direct addition (<5% volume) 15%,
            Natural botanical extract (elderflower, ginger, cucumber) 8%,
            Proprietary flavor compound (IFF / Givaudan / Symrise) 5%,
            Citric acid (tartness adjustment — co-addition) 2% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Natural fruit flavor concentrate
        L0  Fruit volatile / terpene concentrate (CO₂-extracted or spray-dried; food grade)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module (3.0–3.5 vol CO₂ — HIGHEST carbonation of all OTs)
        — alternatives: Industrial bulk CO₂ 72%,
          Recovered fermentation CO₂ 26%,
          CO₂ + nitrogen (rare, specialty) 2% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ food grade ≥99.9% (high purity required for flavor neutrality)
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (CRYSTAL CLEAR — stringent filtration mandatory)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: Cross-flow / membrane (0.2 μm polishing — preferred) 45%,
            Sheet/cartridge filtration (0.2–0.45 μm) 30%,
            Kieselguhr + polishing filter 15%,
            Centrifugal + membrane polish 10% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: None / membrane only (dominant) 45%,
            Bentonite (protein removal) 30%,
            Silica gel / kieselsol 20%,
            PVPP 5% [HIGH]
    L3.8 Antioxidant Treatment (CRITICAL — flavor neutrality demanded)
          — alternatives: Ascorbic acid (L-ascorbic acid; preferred for flavor neutrality) 60%,
            SO₂ / potassium metabisulfite 30%,
            Combined ascorbic + SO₂ 10% [HIGH]
      L0  Ascorbic acid (L-ascorbic acid, food grade)
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly (CAN DOMINANT — 93%)
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum slim can 12 oz / 355 mL (202 diameter) 75%,
            Aluminum standard can 12 oz 10%,
            Aluminum can 16 oz 8%,
            Glass bottle (niche, premium) 5%,
            PET bottle (sports/convenience) 2% [HIGH]
      L2  Slim aluminum can assembly (dominant path — 202 diameter, taller profile)
        L1  Slim can body (drawn/ironed, 202 × 510 or 202 × 412 profile)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 3004-H19 (body stock; thinner gauge for slim profile)
        L1  Slim can end (B64 or CDL, 202 diameter)
          L0  Aluminum alloy sheet 5182-H19
    L3.10 Closure / Seal
          — alternatives: Aluminum pull-tab lid (can) 93%,
            Crown cap (glass bottle) 5%,
            Screw cap (PET) 2% [HIGH]
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1
    L1  Shrink sleeve or direct-print can decoration (full-body graphics preferred)
    L1  4-/6-/12-pack variety pack carrier (corrugated or HDPE)
    L1  24-pack corrugated shipper
```

---

### OT-7: Sour / Wild Ale

Mixed fermentation using Saccharomyces + wild organisms (*Brettanomyces*, *Lactobacillus*, *Pediococcus*). Aged months to years in oak barrels or foudres. Lactic and acetic acid dominate. May include whole fruit additions (kriek, framboise). Spontaneous fermentation (Lambic) is the traditional extreme case; modern American Wild Ale uses controlled pitching. Cork-and-cage 750 mL bottle is a major packaging choice unique to this segment.

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Sour / Wild Ale
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water
      L0  Soft-to-moderately-hard water (Brussels/Bruges profile for Lambic; varies by style)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate
          — alternatives: Pale malt + unmalted wheat (Lambic/Gueuze — Belgian tradition) 35%,
            Pale malt base + malted wheat (American Wild Ale) 30%,
            Pale malt + oats + wheat (Berliner Weisse / Gose) 20%,
            Pale malt + Vienna/Munich malt (Flanders Red / Oud Bruin) 15% [HIGH]
      L2  Malted grain substrate
        L1  Pale barley malt + raw unmalted wheat (Lambic path — 60:40 ratio)
          L0  Barley grain (Hordeum vulgare, pale malt)
          L0  Wheat grain (Triticum aestivum, raw/unmalted — starch for wild enzyme attack)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent (AGED HOPS — antimicrobial function only, not bitterness)
          — alternatives: Aged/oxidized whole cone hops (Lambic: 3+ year old stale hops) 40%,
            Aged hop pellets T-90 (low alpha, antimicrobial only) 30%,
            No hop addition (Gose / salt-acidified variants) 15%,
            Fresh noble pellets T-90 (modern American Wild Ale) 15% [MEDIUM]
      L1  Aged whole cone hops (stale; 3+ years; oxidized bale storage)
        L0  Dried hop cone (Humulus lupulus; alpha-acids degraded to iso-acids for antimicrobial action; no bitterness function)
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent (MIXED CULTURE — distinguishing feature)
          — alternatives: Propagated mixed culture (Brettanomyces + Lactobacillus + Pediococcus) 40%,
            Spontaneous fermentation (ambient wild yeast + bacteria — Lambic) 25%,
            Saccharomyces + kettle-souring Lactobacillus (clean-sour shortcut path) 25%,
            Saccharomyces + Brettanomyces secondary (American Wild Ale) 10% [HIGH]
      L1  Mixed culture pitch (propagated blend)
        L0  Brettanomyces bruxellensis / anomalus culture (volatile acid + phenol production)
        L0  Lactobacillus plantarum culture (lactic acid; pH 3.2–3.8 target)
        L0  Pediococcus damnosus culture (secondary lactic; ropiness intermediate)
    L3.5 Fruit & Flavor Additions (KRIEK / FRAMBOISE / PECHE — major differentiation)
          — alternatives: No fruit (Gueuze, straight Lambic) 40%,
            Whole sour cherries (kriek — Morello / Schaerbeek) 25%,
            Raspberry puree or whole fruit (framboise) 15%,
            Peach puree / whole fruit (pêche) 8%,
            Blackcurrant, elderflower, or other botanicals 7%,
            Salt + coriander (Gose path) 5% [HIGH]
      L1  Whole Morello cherries (kriek — added to aging vessel, 200–400 g/L)
        L0  Morello sour cherry (Prunus cerasus; fresh or frozen whole fruit)
      L1  Raspberry puree / whole raspberry
        L0  Raspberry fruit (Rubus idaeus; crushed puree, food grade)
      L1  Sea salt + whole coriander seeds (Gose path)
        L0  NaCl (sodium chloride, food grade) + Coriandrum sativum seed
  L4.2 Carbonation Module
        — alternatives: Bottle conditioning (residual yeast refermentation — Gueuze tradition) 50%,
          Industrial / recovered CO₂ forced (modern Wild Ale) 35%,
          Cask / keg conditioning (natural draught) 15% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ (fermentation-derived during bottle conditioning, or industrial)
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity (minimal intervention — wild character preserved)
    L3.6 Filtration
          — alternatives: None / unfined (Lambic / Gueuze tradition) 50%,
            Light sheet filtration (commercial wild ale) 30%,
            Centrifugal disc-stack (partial clarification, pre-blend) 15%,
            Kieselguhr (commercial Berliner Weisse) 5% [HIGH]
    L3.7 Fining Agent
          — alternatives: None (wild character / haze accepted) 65%,
            Bentonite (commercial sour) 20%,
            Isinglass 10%,
            Carrageenan 5% [MEDIUM]
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container (750 mL CORK BOTTLE — major unique path)
          — alternatives: 750 mL cork-finished glass bottle (Belgian tradition / premium) 35%,
            Aluminum can 12 oz / 16 oz (US craft sour) 35%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz (US craft sour) 18%,
            Stainless keg (draught Berliner Weisse, Gose) 12% [HIGH]
      L2  750 mL champagne-profile glass bottle assembly
        L1  750 mL amber or clear champagne-weight bottle (pressure-rated)
          L0  Soda-lime-silica glass (champagne weight; high internal pressure rating)
    L3.10 Closure / Seal (CORK + CAGE — unique to this output type)
          — alternatives: Cork + wire cage / muselet (750 mL Belgian tradition) 35%,
            Aluminum pull-tab lid (can) 35%,
            Crown cap 26 mm (12 oz bottle) 18%,
            Keg spear + coupler 12% [HIGH]
      L1  Natural cork stopper (agglomerated or colmated, 24 × 38 mm champagne profile)
        L0  Cork (Quercus suber bark; granulated + polyurethane binder for agglomerate)
      L1  Aluminum wire cage (muselet, 5-turn, ~30-gauge wire)
        L0  Aluminum wire (low-carbon ductile; Ø ~1.5 mm)
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1
    L1  Wet-glue paper label (Belgian artisanal tradition) or pressure-sensitive
    L1  Tissue wrap (premium 750 mL presentation — specialty path)
    L1  6-pack corrugated or 12-pack case
```

---

### OT-8: Seasonal / Specialty Beer

Defined by seasonal ingredients added to an otherwise conventional base style. Core sub-categories: (a) Pumpkin / Gourd beer; (b) Winter Warmer (spiced strong ale); (c) Märzen / Oktoberfest (amber lager); (d) Summer Shandy / Radler (beer + citrus); (e) Barrel-Aged Specialty. The L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly inherits from OT-1 or OT-2. **L3.5 Specialty Ingredient Module is the differentiating BOM position.**

```
OUTPUT TYPE: Seasonal / Specialty Beer
  L4.1 Fermented Liquid Assembly
    L3.1 Base Water — inherits from base style (soft for wheat-based, sulfate for hop-forward)
    L3.2 Fermentable Substrate (season-modified)
          — alternatives: Vienna/Munich malt blend (Märzen/Oktoberfest — amber lager) 30%,
            Dark malt + oats + base malt (Winter Warmer / Holiday ale) 25%,
            Base malt + pumpkin puree (Pumpkin beer — gourd as adjunct) 20%,
            Standard pale malt (Summer Shandy base — ale or lager) 15%,
            High-gravity imperial bill (barrel-aged base) 10% [HIGH]
      L2  Malt substrate (Märzen path)
        L1  Vienna malt (5–9 EBC) + Munich malt (10–25 EBC)
          L0  Barley grain (Vienna kilning: higher temperature → Maillard melanoidins)
      L2  Pumpkin adjunct (Pumpkin beer path)
        L1  Cooked pumpkin puree (added to mash or whirlpool)
          L0  Cucurbita pepo (pumpkin / butternut squash; cooked puree, food grade)
    L3.3 Bittering Agent (inherits from base style)
          — alternatives: Noble hops (Märzen/lager base) 40%,
            English hops (Winter Warmer / spiced ale) 30%,
            American hops (Summer seasonal / IPA variant) 25%,
            Minimal/no hops (Shandy base — diluted with juice post-filter) 5% [HIGH]
    L3.4 Fermentation Agent (inherits from base style)
          — alternatives: S. pastorianus lager (Märzen) 35%,
            S. cerevisiae ale (Winter Warmer, Pumpkin, Summer ale) 50%,
            Belgian S. cerevisiae (strong seasonal, Tripel-style) 12%,
            Mixed culture (barrel-aged specialty) 3% [HIGH]
    L3.5 Specialty Ingredient Module (DISTINGUISHING MODULE)
      Sub-type A — Pumpkin Beer:
          — alternatives: Pumpkin puree (mash/whirlpool adjunct) 50%,
            Gourd spice blend alone (cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, clove) 30%,
            Pumpkin puree + spice blend (combined) 15%,
            Artificial pumpkin flavor (cost-reduction path) 5% [MEDIUM]
        L1  Cooked pumpkin puree (retort-packed or IQF)
          L0  Cucurbita pepo (pumpkin; 100% gourd flesh, food grade)
        L1  Spice blend (cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, clove, ginger — dry, whole or ground)
          L0  Ground cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum bark)
          L0  Ground nutmeg (Myristica fragrans seed)
          L0  Ground allspice (Pimenta dioica berry)
          L0  Ground clove (Syzygium aromaticum flower bud)
      Sub-type B — Winter Warmer:
          — alternatives: Spice blend (cinnamon, ginger, orange peel, star anise) 55%,
            Vanilla addition (extract or whole bean) 25%,
            Honey (late addition, partially fermentable) 15%,
            No spice (unspiced strong dark ale) 5% [MEDIUM]
        L1  Whole cinnamon stick + star anise (whirlpool or dry-spice addition)
          L0  Cinnamomum verum bark (whole stick)
          L0  Illicium verum (whole dried star anise pod)
        L1  Vanilla extract or whole bean
          L0  Vanillin / vanilla extract (Vanilla planifolia; alcohol-extracted or synthetic)
      Sub-type C — Märzen / Oktoberfest:
          No specialty additions — flavor derived from L3.2 Vienna/Munich malt selection only.
      Sub-type D — Summer Shandy / Radler:
          — alternatives: Lemon/citrus juice concentrate (post-filter 30–50% blend) 60%,
            Grapefruit juice concentrate 20%,
            Natural lemon flavor (artificial/natural blend) 15%,
            Elderflower / botanical mix 5% [MEDIUM]
        L1  Lemon juice concentrate (food grade, Brix 50–60)
          L0  Lemon juice (Citrus limon; cold-press concentrate; citric acid + limonene)
  L4.2 Carbonation Module
        — alternatives: Industrial / recovered CO₂ (same as base style) 95%,
          Bottle conditioning (seasonal farmhouse / Belgian variant) 5% [HIGH]
    L0  CO₂ food grade
  L4.3 Stabilization & Clarity — inherits from base style (see OT-1 or OT-2)
  L4.4 Primary Packaging Assembly
    L3.9 Primary Container
          — alternatives: Aluminum can 12 oz / 16 oz (seasonal 4-pack / variety) 55%,
            Glass bottle 12 oz / 22 oz (seasonal gift / bomber) 30%,
            Stainless keg (draught seasonal tap) 12%,
            750 mL glass bottle (barrel-aged specialty — cork+cage path) 3% [HIGH]
    L3.10 Closure / Seal — inherits from container choice (see OT-1 through OT-7 per path)
  L4.5 Label & Secondary Packaging — Stop at L1
    L1  Seasonal specialty label (limited-edition design; OPP or paper)
    L1  4-pack seasonal variety carrier or 6-pack corrugated
    L1  Gift box / paper sleeve (barrel-aged specialty path)
    L1  24-pack corrugated shipper
```
